name: Sidebar Layout Intent
summary: A primitive layout region-role (#1680 taxonomy): composition-intent = a fixed + fluid pair that collapses to a stack when narrow. Identity is the arrangement, not the CSS — FUI ships one block per role and the mechanism (flex with flex-basis + flex-grow + wrap) is an impl seam. Project-less; per-project styling rides presentational variants + plateau assembler presets.

side.description: Which of the two regions is the fixed-width one. Default start.

side-width.description: The fixed region's basis (its intrinsic or set inline-size). Absent → the region's intrinsic content width.

content-min.description: The minimum inline-size (a percentage) the fluid region must keep before the pair collapses to a stack — the no-breakpoint reflow threshold.

gap.description: Even spacing between the two regions, on a spacing scale.

description:
What This Is

Two adjacent regions — one of fixed/intrinsic width, one fluid — that sit side by side until the fluid region would shrink past a threshold, at which point they collapse to a stack. The fixed+fluid split.

Composition-intent (the identity)

A primitive layout region-role (layoutTier: region-role), one of the per-role taxonomy ratified in #1680. Its identity is the composition-intent — a fixed + fluid pair that collapses to a stack when narrow — impl-agnostic. FUI ships exactly one block for this role; the CSS mechanism (flex with flex-basis + flex-grow + wrap) is an impl seam, never the role's identity (platform-decisions.md#layout-role-composition-intent).

Dimensions (UX-only)

  • side: Which of the two regions is the fixed-width one. Default start (start · end).
  • side-width: The fixed region's basis (its intrinsic or set inline-size). Absent → the region's intrinsic content width.
  • content-min: The minimum inline-size (a percentage) the fluid region must keep before the pair collapses to a stack — the no-breakpoint reflow threshold.
  • gap: Even spacing between the two regions, on a spacing scale (none · xs · sm · md · lg · xl).

Annotation, not identity

The draggable boundary is the existing resizable intent (#1384), composed — not part of this role. An optional navigation | complementary landmark is a content annotation.
